Rugby.title: Rassie Erasmus defends Bok star after 'worst player on the pitch' jibe
Rugby.summary: Rassie Erasmus has dismissed criticism of experienced outside half Handré Pollard after the 32-year-old struggled in the URC playoffs for the Bulls, which saw them lose 36-7 in the final to Leinster.
Rugby.body:
Rassie Erasmus has dismissed criticism of experienced outside half Handré Pollard after the 32-year-old struggled in the URC playoffs for the Bulls, which saw them lose 36-7 in the final to Leinster.

His support is timely given the flak aimed his way by former Irish star and now leading pundit Simon Zebo, who stated after the final: “Pollard, a World Cup winner. He’s been the worst player on the pitch. Canan Moodie, you could say the same thing about him. You rely on your best players to step up in big games. They have been nothing short of horrible.”

Rugby.title: Cullen to leave Leinster at end of next season
Rugby.summary: Leinster head coach Leo Cullen is to leave his role with the Irish province at the end of next season.
Rugby.body:
Leo Cullen is to leave his role as Leinster head coach at the end of next season. The former Ireland second row will depart after 12 years in the position with the Irish province when his contract concludes at the end of the 2026-27 campaign, a statement from Leinster confirmed on Monday.

The 48-year-old has led Leinster to six league titles during his tenure, the latest coming in the United Rugby Championship decider against the Bulls on Friday. He also guided the province to a European Champions Cup success in 2018 but his side subsequently lost five finals in the competition, including four in the past five years.

Rugby.title: All Blacks call-up Fehi Fineanganofo casts major doubt on Newcastle Red Bulls move
Rugby.summary: Fehi Fineanganofo has cast major doubt over his impending move to the Newcastle Red Bulls following his recent selection for the All Blacks.
Rugby.body:
Fehi Fineanganofo has cast major doubt over his impending move to the Newcastle Red Bulls following his recent selection for the All Blacks. The 23-year-old Hurricanes winger, who enjoyed a breakthrough season by equalling the Super Rugby Pacific record with 17 tries, was named as one of four uncapped players in Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad for the upcoming Nations Championship.

Fineanganofo had originally signed a two-year deal with the Gallagher PREM side earlier this year, a move that was expected to pause his international ambitions in New Zealand.

Rugby.title: Firefighting prop Scoble set to play last Wales game
Rugby.summary: Firefighter Jenni Scoble is set to play her final game for Wales against the Barbarians at Twickenham on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Firefighting Wales prop Jenni Scoble will retire from international rugby after the fixture against Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday. The 33-year-old is set to make one last outing for her country in the double-header in Twickenham, with the women's game taking place at 17:00 BST after the men play at 14:00 BST.

Scoble will continue to play for Gwalia Lightning in the Celtic Challenge but has opted to retire from international rugby to focus on her job as a firefighter. "This has been an incredibly tough decision to make and is one I have thought long and hard about," said Scoble.

Rugby.title: Prem Rugby play-offs set to move to neutral venue
Rugby.summary: The Prem Rugby play-offs are set to move from home advantage to a neutral venue from the 2029-2030 season.
Rugby.body:
The Prem Rugby play-offs are set be played at a neutral venue from the 2029-2030 season. The current format, which sees the top two teams in the regular season table getting home advantage in the semi-finals, has been in place since 2005.

But league bosses say they have a vision of playing both the play-offs on consecutive days in one city, similar to the European finals weekend. It is thought Liverpool and Brighton are two of the cities under consideration at this early stage.

Rugby.title: Tamati Ellison names 26-man Maori All Blacks squad for Japan XV clash
Rugby.summary: Ellison said that naming his first squad is a proud moment, and that all of the players have earned their opportunity.
Rugby.body:
Maori All Blacks head coach Tamati Ellison has named his 26 man squad for this weekend’s encounter with Japan XV at Paloma Mizuho Stadium, in Nagoya. The former All Blacks assistant, who left his role alongside Scott Robertson, Bryn Evans and Scott Hansen, has picked a strong squad to head to Japan, helped by assistant coaches Greg Feek (Kahungunu) and Marty Bourke (Tainui).

Ellison has named six uncapped players as part of the 2026 squad. The possible debutants include, JJ Pokai, Reon Paul, Torian Barnes, Adam Lennox, Payton Spencer and Tahlor Cahill.

Rugby.title: All Blacks head coach details what made Anton Segner impossible to leave out
Rugby.summary: "We think he could generally be a high quality seven, but you know he's got a really balanced skill set."
Rugby.body:
The big inclusion in Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad of 2026 was German-born Blues loose-forward Anton Segner, who had a standout season for the Auckland-based franchise.

The Blues loose-forward is versatile, can play all three positions in the back-row, and his form in the 2026 Super Rugby Pacific season has given him an opportunity in Rennie’s first squad.

Rugby.title: Tandy's Wales prepare for challenging summer of travel
Rugby.summary: Welsh domestic rugby might have finished at the end of May but there is very little rest for the Wales national men's side this summer.
Rugby.body:
Welsh domestic rugby might have finished at the end of May but there is very little rest for the national men's side. Steve Tandy's Wales team face four matches in successive weekends with the rugby season lasting until mid-July.

The uncapped men's and women's double-header against Barbarians on 27 June at the Allianz Stadium, will be followed by three games in the new Nations Championship against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa in July.

Rugby.title: Dave Rennie on the Hurricanes' pair of wingers and the four potential debutants
Rugby.summary: Dave Rennie's first All Blacks squad includes four potential debutants, including three from the Super Rugby champion Hurricanes squad.
Rugby.body:
Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad includes four potential debutants, including three from the Super Rugby champion Hurricanes squad. Blues loose forward Anton Segner, Hurricanes prop Xavier Numia, and the pair of record-setting Hurricanes wingers Fehi Fineanganofo and Josh Moorby are the four rookies in line to become All Blacks.

Fineanganofo and Moorby set a new single season try scoring record with 17 tries each in the record-setting championship season. Doubt still surrounds Fehi Fineanganofo’s long-term future in New Zealand, but while he is contracted to NZR until the end of November, remains eligible to play.

Rugby.title: Ex-All Blacks left shocked over Du'Plessis Kirifi omission from All Blacks squad
Rugby.summary: Ex-All Blacks James Parsons and Jeff Wilson have been left shocked at the omission of Hurricanes openside flanker Du'Plessis Kirifi.
Rugby.body:
Ex-All Blacks James Parsons and Jeff Wilson have been left shocked at the omission of Hurricanes openside flanker Du’Plessis Kirifi in the first All Blacks squad of 2026. After the co-captain lead the Hurricanes to a comprehensive 60-5 win over the Chiefs in the Super Rugby Pacific final, Kirifi was one of the notable omissions from Dave Rennie’s first squad.

Parsons said that Kirifi “could not have done anymore” as a player after his most impressive season yet with the Hurricanes, and the selection of Savea as captain suggests that he will be an 80-minute player.

Rugby.title: Dave Rennie addresses Fehi Fineanganofo's future after All Blacks call-up
Rugby.summary: "His form has been irresistible, and we're keen to name him."
Rugby.body:
New All Blacks head coach Dave Rennie has given the public an insight into what conversations have been had with Hurricanes winger Fehi Fineanganofo. Fineanganofo, who scored 17 tries in the 2026 Super Rugby Pacific season, was named in Rennie’s first All Blacks squad on Monday afternoon.

The Hurricanes winger showed impressive form throughout the Super Rugby season, alongside his teammate, Josh Moorby, who was also selected as an uncapped player in Rennie’s squad.

Rugby.title: Why Rennie chose Ardie Savea as the new All Blacks captain
Rugby.summary: Ardie Savea will be the new All Blacks captain after incoming head coach Dave Rennie named the talismanic figure as his captain at the 2026 Nations Championship squad announcement.
Rugby.body:
Ardie Savea will be the new All Blacks captain after incoming head coach Dave Rennie named the talismanic figure as his captain at the 2026 Nations Championship squad announcement in Fielding.

Savea will led the 34-man squad into the July Nations Championship fixtures against France, Italy and Ireland. The 32-year-old officially takes over from Scott Barrett, although Savea has plenty of on-field experience with the role as a vice-captain under former coaches Ian Foster and Scott Robertson.

Rugby.title: Dave Rennie unveils first All Blacks squad ahead of Nations Championship Tests
Rugby.summary: The All Blacks will travel to Christchurch to take on France, before facing Italy in Wellington, and Ireland in Auckland.
Rugby.body:
All Blacks head coach Dave Rennie has named his 34-man squad for three July Nations Championship fixtures, starting in less than two weeks time against France at One New Zealand Stadium in Christchurch.

Rennie and his coaching staff have named Ardie Savea as the new All Blacks captain, replacing Scott Barrett, who remains out of action with a serious back injury. Rennie, who was appointed as head coach of the All Blacks back in March, has named four uncapped players, Anton Segner, Xavier Numia, Josh Moorby, and Fehi Fineanganofo.

Rennie, who was appointed as head coach of the All Blacks back in March, has named four uncapped players, Anton Segner, Xavier Numia, Josh Moorby, and Fehi Fineanganofo.

Rugby.title: How the Hurricanes rebuilt the roster to produce this masterpiece title
Rugby.summary: The Hurricanes became Super Rugby champions for the second time in history with the most complete final performance in the competition's history, beating the Chiefs by 60-5.
Rugby.body:
The Hurricanes became Super Rugby champions for the second time in history with the most complete final performance in the competition’s history, beating the Chiefs by 60-5. This championship took 10 years to build, with a combination of homegrown talent and savvy recruitment decisions building a unique squad that has quite literally blown away opposition.

It has been a remarkable year and the 2026 Hurricanes have a strong claim as the greatest Super Rugby team in history. They are the first ever to crack 700 points in a season, while their 55-point margin in a final is the most ever. They were the fastest club to notch 100 tries around the world in 2026.

Rugby.title: Force teenager shocks Reds, Drua overcome Brumbies
Rugby.summary: The 23-year-old was left gripping her leg with an agonising grimace on her face before she gingerly hobbled off the field with the support of doctors.
Rugby.body:
Teenage debutant Ai Dickson has stepped up in the Western Force’s moment of need to secure a stirring 21-17 comeback victory against last year’s Super Rugby Women grand finalists Queensland.

The Force were looking to pick up their first win of the season and boost their hopes of a finals berth at Perth’s Wanneroo Rugby Club on Saturday. But their victory aspirations looked in doubt as they trailed 17-7 at halftime.

Rugby.title: Former Ireland Sevens star Chay Mullins inks deal with Ulster
Rugby.summary: Former Ireland Sevens star Chay Mullins is set to continue his rise in the longer format of the game.
Rugby.body:
Former Ireland Sevens star Chay Mullins is set to continue his rise in the longer format of the game, the 24-year-old confirming a one-year deal with URC side Ulster. Mullins enjoyed a decorated career in the Irish 7s program, making the squad for the 2024 Olympic Games in Paris, and winning a bronze medal at the Sevens World Cup in 2022.

He has also made it to the higher levels of Irish representation in the past, also playing for the Ireland U20s and working with Richie Murphy, who has since transitioned into Ulster’s head coaching role.

Rugby.title: ‘What a story it would be’: Abbie Brown eyes 2028 Olympics with GB
Rugby.summary: Abbie Brown is hoping to use the experience gained from the 2026 season to push a new generation forward.
Rugby.body:
The 2025-26 HSBC SVNS Series proved a difficult one for Great Britain, the side becoming the first team to be relegated from the first division under the new three-tier model.

However, veteran Abbie Brown is hoping to use the experience gained from the 2026 season to push a new generation forward, with an eye on getting Team GB back for the 2028 LA Olympics.

Rugby.title: Henry Pollock: 'People can talk all they want... It doesn’t matter to me'
Rugby.summary: England star Henry Pollock has warned South Africa he will respond to any wind-up attempts by delivering on the field.
Rugby.body:
England star Henry Pollock has warned South Africa he will respond to any wind-up attempts by delivering on the field. Pollock was instrumental in Northampton winning the Gallagher Prem title on Saturday, with his man-of-the-match performance against Exeter at Allianz Stadium arriving eight days after he also starred in the play-off against Leicester.

The flamboyant 21-year-old has played his way into a prominent role when England launch their July tour in Johannesburg on July 4, but he knows he is an unpopular figure in South Africa.

Rugby.title: 'I'm worried': Rassie Erasmus issues update on injured Bok
Rugby.summary: Rassie Erasmus has confirmed that Franco Mostert will undergo scans after the lock was forced off during the Springboks' 80-31 victory over the Barbarians in Gqeberha on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Rassie Erasmus has confirmed that Franco Mostert will undergo scans after the lock was forced off during the Springboks’ 80-31 victory over the Barbarians in Gqeberha on Saturday.

The Boks ran in 12 tries at the Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium in their first outing of the season, with the Barbarians, assembled at short notice with only three training sessions together, offering little in the way of organised resistance.

Rugby.title: Major omissions from provisional France Nations Championship squad as Penaud re-called
Rugby.summary: Fabien Galthié has named a 39-man provisional France squad for the Nations Championship, with the final group to be confirmed by Wednesday...
Rugby.body:
Fabien Galthié has named a 39-man provisional France squad for the Nations Championship, with the final group to be confirmed by Wednesday, June 24 ahead of a southern hemisphere tour that takes in Tests against New Zealand, Australia and Japan.

The headline story is the return of Damian Penaud. France’s all-time leading try-scorer was notably absent for the Six Nations earlier this year, but the Bordeaux-Bègles man is back in the fold after a prolific club season in which he scored 11 tries in 29 appearances, often from centre, where he was deployed following Nicolas Depoortere’s injury.

Rugby.title: Feyi-Waboso risked England place for Exeter return
Rugby.summary: Exeter winger Immanuel Feyi-Waboso says he had no choice but to risk his England chances this summer by playing in the Prem final only three weeks after breaking his jaw.
Rugby.body:
Exeter winger Immanuel Feyi-Waboso has said he had no choice but to risk his England chances this summer by playing in the Prem final only three weeks after suffering a broken jaw.

The 23-year-old had surgery having sustained the injury in Exeter's win at Leicester at the end of May but was cleared to play in Saturday's defeat by Northampton. Feyi-Waboso said the rapid turnaround was worth the gamble of potentially missing out on featuring in England's Nations Championship Tests against South Africa, Fiji and Argentina.

Rugby.title: Five Springboks who may never play for South Africa again
Rugby.summary: The Springboks have named a 46-man squad for the 2026 Nations Championship that is as deep and as loaded as any Rassie Erasmus has assembled.
Rugby.body:
The Springboks have named a 46-man squad for the 2026 Nations Championship that is – with the exception of the injured Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu – as deep and as loaded as any Rassie Erasmus has assembled.

With England, Scotland and Wales all to come before the Greatest Rivalry Series against the All Blacks, South Africa’s embarrassment of riches across every position is plain to see. But with that depth comes brutal selection reality.

Tennis.title: Sinner opens play on day one
Tennis.summary: Wimbledon begins on Monday, 29 June - and you can follow every minute live across the BBC.
Tennis.body:
Wimbledon begins on Monday - and you can follow every minute live across the BBC. World number one Jannik Sinner will be defending his men's singles crown, with rival and two-time winner Carlos Alcaraz missing with a wrist injury.

Poland's Iga Swiatek is the defending women's champion, having beaten Amanda Anisimova 6-0 6-0 in last year's showpiece. The tournament takes place from Monday, 29 June to Sunday, 11 July, with coverage across all BBC platforms.

Tennis.title: Draper tackles tough Wimbledon test boosted by Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Draper tackles tough Wimbledon test boosted by Eastbourne The Argus
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper is ready to embrace the challenge of facing world number seven Taylor Fritz after the “lottery” of his unseeded status handed him a daunting Wimbledon opener. The 24-year-old was at the mercy of the draw for his home grand slam, having plummeted down the rankings following an injury-ravaged year.

He was paired with sixth-seeded American Fritz before his competitive warm-up for the championships ended with a 7-5 6-3 defeat by Ugo Humbert in the Lexus Eastbourne Open semi-finals.

Tennis.title: Old guard boosts Wimbledon in battle for centre stage
Tennis.summary: Serena Williams, Jannik Sinner and Novak Djokovic feature in our essential guide to Wimbledon 2026.
Tennis.body:
The Williams sisters, Andy Murray and Novak Djokovic are back at Wimbledon. What year is it again? The mention of those names may make it feel like we're stuck in a timewarp - but, no, it is 2026 and some of the sport's greatest are returning to SW19, in one way or another.

American icon Serena Williams, 44, is making a blockbuster singles comeback after four years away from the sport and also resumes her iconic doubles pairing with sister Venus, 46.

Tennis.title: Will the weather stay dry for week one of Wimbledon?
Tennis.summary: While it will remain warmer than average, the heat and humidity will be a lot less intense than of late
Tennis.body:
The grass is being measured, the rackets strung and the strawberries and cream prepped. But what will the great British weather serve up this year for Wimbledon fortnight? Following a record-breaking June heatwave earlier in the week, temperatures have been dipping through the weekend.

It will be cooler and more unsettled for the first week of Wimbledon 2026. But there will also be plenty of warm sunshine at times too. Monday is looking ideal for most spectators and players.

Tennis.title: The Wimbledon draw could have been kinder to Jack Draper, says Gigi Salmon
Tennis.summary: The Wimbledon draw could have been kinder to Jack Draper, says Gigi Salmon Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Gigi previews Wimbledon with a total of 21 Britons in singles action. The Sky Sports presenter also looks forward to the return of 23-time major winner Serena Williams at the All England Club and picks her winners! It's all in her latest column...

I'm writing this around 3,500 miles away from the All England Club and the Wimbledon Championships, having just spent the afternoon watching a former Wimbledon champion play on a grass-court in New York's Central Park.

Tennis.title: Players 'don't want to boycott again' over prize money
Tennis.summary: Four-time major champion Aryna Sabalenka says she hopes the world's leading players will not have to boycott their Grand Slam media duties again over prize money protests.
Tennis.body:
Four-time major champion Aryna Sabalenka says she hopes the world's leading players will not have to "boycott" their Grand Slam media duties again after Wimbledon. Sabalenka is among the stars who have decided to limit media appearances to 15 minutes at the All England Club this week in protest over the prize money on offer at Grand Slam tournaments.

A group of players, which also includes Jannik Sinner and Coco Gauff, want the major tournaments to commit a greater share of their revenue to prize money, as well as an increased contribution to welfare benefits including pensions and maternity leave.

Tennis.title: Will Emma Raducanu miss Wimbledon? What we know about her injury
Tennis.summary: Will Emma Raducanu miss Wimbledon? What we know about her injury The i Paper
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu is once again facing a race against time to be fit for Wimbledon. The 23-year-old made her breakthrough at SW19 five years ago, reaching the fourth round as a teenager, and achieved the same in 2024.

Only world No 1 Aryna Sabalenka stopped her in a Friday night barnburner last year, and hopes were high coming to Wimbledon after Raducanu reached the final of Queen’s. But in keeping with the injury-dogged nature of her career thus far, she has not been able to get back on the match court since – and the No 30 seed is in danger of failing to do so for her first-round match against Antonia Ruzic on Monday.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu cuts short training session to deepen fears of Wimbledon withdrawal
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu cuts short training session to deepen fears of Wimbledon withdrawal The Guardian
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu’s presence at Wimbledon remains shrouded in doubt after the British No 1 was forced to cut short her training session at the All England Club two days before her opening match.

Raducanu, who had been managing a right foot injury since her run to the final at Queen’s Club, had not trained for the past two days after being spotted leaving the venue wearing a medical boot on her right foot.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu: Wimbledon fitness concerns grow as British No 1 cuts short practice session
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu: Wimbledon fitness concerns grow as British No 1 cuts short practice session Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu cut short her practice session at Wimbledon before cancelling a scheduled press conference as concerns about her fitness deepened. The British No 1 has been at the centre of another injury scare since she was reportedly seen wearing a protective boot on Wednesday evening.

Raducanu did not train at Wimbledon on Thursday or Friday but was down for two hours of hitting on Saturday, first at the All England Club's Aorangi Park practice courts and then on Court Three with Russia's Anna Kalinskaya.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper questions early let call after Eastbourne semi-final defeat to Ugo Humbert
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper questions early let call after Eastbourne semi-final defeat to Ugo Humbert SussexWorld
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper insisted an early disputed let call had no bearing on his Lexus Eastbourne Open semi-final defeat to Ugo Humbert – but admitted he was left confused by the decision.

The former world number four saw his impressive run at Devonshire Park come to an end, with a 7-5 6-3 semi-final defeat to the Frenchman on Friday (June 26). One talking point came in Humbert’s opening service game when, at 30-30, play was stopped after the chair umpire called a let. This was apparently due to noise from a speaker.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu postpones pre-Wimbledon press conference as injury fears grow
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu postpones pre-Wimbledon press conference as injury fears grow The Independent
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu postponed her pre-Wimbledon press conference after cutting a practice session short, increasing fears that the British No 1 could be forced to this year’s tournament through injury.

Raducanu has been managing a foot problem since reaching the Queen’s final this month and appeared at the All England Club on Saturday with her right ankle strapped in protective tape.

Tennis.title: Raducanu quits practice as Wimbledon fitness fears grow
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu's chances of being fit for Wimbledon look to be in considerable doubt after the Briton cut short her practice on Saturday.
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu's chances of being fit for Wimbledon look to be in considerable doubt after the British number one cut short her practice session on Saturday. The 23-year-old postponed her pre-tournament media shortly after quitting a practice set against Russian Anna Kalinskaya with 10 minutes remaining.

Raducanu, who is seeded 30th at the All England Club, is due to start her campaign on Monday against Croatia's Antonia Ruzic on Court One (13:00 BST). The 2021 US Open champion did not train on Thursday and Friday because of what is believed to be a shin problem, but returned to the court on Saturday to test her fitness.

Tennis.title: Keys beats Maria for third Eastbourne title
Tennis.summary: Second seed Madison Keys secures her third Eastbourne title with 7-5 6-4 win over Tatjana Maria.
Tennis.body:
Madison Keys secured her third Eastbourne title with a straight-sets win over Tatjana Maria. Keys beat German Maria 7-5 6-4 to join tennis legends Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova as the only players to win the women's tournament on three or more occasions.

The American first triumphed in Eastbourne in 2014 - her first WTA title - before a second success came in 2023. "I always forget how heavy this one is," Keys, 31, joked as she lifted the trophy.

Tennis.title: Sinner seeks to resume dominance at Wimbledon after Paris shock
Tennis.summary: Jannik Sinner is the strong favourite to retain his Wimbledon title in the absence of rival Carlos Alcaraz, despite his shock early loss at the French Open.
Tennis.body:
For the past two years, Jannik Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz have stood a class above the rest at the top of men's tennis. And so, once his rival and good friend succumbed to a wrist injury in April, world number one Sinner appeared almost entirely untouchable.

But Sinner would fall victim to one of the biggest shocks at a major in recent years at the French Open, exiting in the second round despite leading Argentine Juan Manuel Cerundolo by two sets and 5-1 in the third.

Tennis.title: Bad Homburg: Naomi Osaka retires during final with foot issue vs Karolina Muchova prompting Wimbledon concern
Tennis.summary: Bad Homburg: Naomi Osaka retires during final with foot issue vs Karolina Muchova prompting Wimbledon concern Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Naomi Osaka's Wimbledon preparations received a setback when she retired at the start of the second set in Saturday's Bad Homburg final because of a foot injury. The fourth-seeded Japanese player, in her first grass-court final, was trailing 6-1 1-0 to Karolina Muchova of the Czech Republic when she pulled out.

Osaka had taken a medical timeout in the first set. Then, after gesturing she could not continue, she walked over to shake hands with Muchova and the umpire. - Not got Sky? Get Sky Sports or stream with no contract

Tennis.title: Wimbledon build-up: News, updates, with Emma Raducanu, Jack Draper, Jannik Sinner, Aryna Sabalenka, Serena Williams preparing for Grand Slam
Tennis.summary: Wimbledon build-up: News, updates, with Emma Raducanu, Jack Draper, Jannik Sinner, Aryna Sabalenka, Serena Williams preparing for Grand Slam Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Based on the split of the draw, second seed and French Open champion Alexander Zverev looks to have come out the best. Top seed and defending champion Jannik Sinner has Novak Djokovic, Daniil Medvedev and third seed Felix Auger-Aliassime on his side.

For Zverev, the top seeds on his side are American duo Ben Shelton and Taylor Fritz, and Australia's Alex de Minaur. Novak Djokovic, seventh seed at Wimbledon this year, has drawn China's Yibing Wu first up.

Tennis.title: Draper denied place in Eastbourne final by Humbert
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper misses out on a place in the Eastbourne Open final after Frenchman Ugo Humbert ends the Briton's encouraging return to action following injury.
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper missed out on a place in the Eastbourne final as Ugo Humbert ended the Briton's encouraging return to competition following injury. Draper, 24, had earned three consecutive straight-set wins to reach the semi-finals at his first tournament in more than two months, after he sustained a knee injury at the Barcelona Open in April.

The former world number four gained more valuable time on court before turning his attention to Wimbledon, but he lost 7-5 6-3 after 100 minutes in sapping conditions against the 30th-ranked Humbert.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper's path to Wimbledon glory
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper's path to Wimbledon glory Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper's daunting Wimbledon draw was followed by disappointment at the Lexus Eastbourne Open as he suffered a semi-final exit at the hands of Ugo Humbert. The 24-year-old discovered on Friday morning he will face world No 7 Taylor Fritz in the first round at the All England Club after an injury-ravaged year left him unseeded.

He was then denied a place in the final at Devonshire Park as he slipped to a 7-5 6-3 loss to sixth seed Humbert in an hour and 42 minutes. - Not got Sky? Get Sky Sports or stream with no contract

Tennis.title: Lexus Eastbourne Open 2026: Ugo Humbert beats Jack Draper to set up final with Zizou Bergs
Tennis.summary: Lexus Eastbourne Open 2026: Ugo Humbert beats Jack Draper to set up final with Zizou Bergs LTA
Tennis.body:
Ugo Humbert and Zizou Bergs will contest the Lexus Eastbourne Open men’s singles final on Saturday, both coming through against British stars Jack Draper and Toby Samuel. It was the first time two British players had reached the semi-finals of the men’s draw since it was held in Eastbourne, but their French and Belgian opponents had the extra edge on the day.

Last week’s HSBC Championships semi-finalist Humbert produced a solid performance to knock out former world No.4 Draper 7-5, 6-3. Meanwhile, Samuel had the chance to serve for the match against Bergs before bowing out 4-6, 7-6(5), 6-2 in two hours and eight minutes.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper's Eastbourne run ended by inspired Ugo Humbert ahead of Wimbledon
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper's Eastbourne run ended by inspired Ugo Humbert ahead of Wimbledon SussexWorld
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper's impressive run at the Lexus Eastbourne Open came to an end on Friday (June 26), after the Brit was beaten in straight sets by an inspired Ugo Humbert. The Frenchman produced a superb display to secure a 7-5 6-3 victory at Devonshire Park, denying Draper a place in the final of the ATP 250 event, on his return from injury.

Draper had arrived in the last four full of confidence after an encouraging week on the south coast, but Humbert proved the sharper player on the day, striking the ball cleanly throughout and taking his chances at key moments.

Tennis.summary: Wimbledon draw throws up mouthwatering Taylor Fritz clash for Jack Draper; Serena Williams also faces a defending Eastbourne champion SussexWorld
Tennis.body:
The countdown to Wimbledon is under way after the draw for this year's Championships was made, with players from Sussex and Surrey discovering the opponents standing between them and a place in the second round.

There will be plenty of local interest when play begins at the All England Club next week, with Brighton's Alicia Dudeney, Burgess Hill & Brighton-linked Julian Cash, and Surrey's Jack Draper & Jack Pinnington Jones all involved.

There will be plenty of local interest when play begins at the All England Club next week, with Brighton's Alicia Dudeney, Burgess Hill & Brighton-linked Julian Cash, and Surrey's Jack Draper & Jack Pinnington Jones all involved.

Tennis.title: Wimbledon: Emma Raducanu and Jack Draper receive tough Wimbledon draws, as Serena Williams learns comeback opponent
Tennis.summary: Wimbledon: Emma Raducanu and Jack Draper receive tough Wimbledon draws, as Serena Williams learns comeback opponent Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu could face world No 1 Aryna Sabalenka in the Wimbledon third round after the draw for the Grand Slam was made on Friday. First 30th seed Raducanu, who remains an injury doubt for the grasscourt major which starts on Monday, must come through a tricky opponent in the opening round as she faces Croatia's Antonia Ruzic.

Jack Draper was also handed a tough opening match against No 6 seed Taylor Fritz. The pair have faced each other five times previously on the ATP Tour, with Draper edging their head-to-head.

Tennis.title: The secret weapon behind Jack Draper's Wimbledon 2026 return
Tennis.summary: The secret weapon behind Jack Draper's Wimbledon 2026 return Team GB
Tennis.body:
Anointed as the next saviour of British tennis following a superb run to the 2024 US Open semi-finals aged just 22, Jack Draper had the world at his feet. The uncertainty of the post-Sir Andy Murray world had been vanquished by a fresh-faced, 6ft 4in Londoner with a penchant for rocketing forehands past unassuming opponents.

At Wimbledon 13 years ago, Murray gave us all a ‘I know where I was’ moment, perhaps the jewel in a career that yielded three majors and two Olympic golds. For over 15 years, the hopes and dreams of a nation rested on the shoulders of the now retired 39-year-old as it had on Tim Henman before him.

The full draw for the women's singles tournament at Wimbledon plus the latest results. Iga Swiatek defends her Wimbledon crown, with world No 1 Aryna Sabalenka, French Open champion Mirra Andreeva and Elena Rybakina among those looking to challenge.

Britain's Emma Raducanu enters seeded after a recent return to form, while Katie Boulter too will seek a run on home soil. Tennis legend Serena Williams makes her Grand Slam singles return as a wild card at the age of 44.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu faces brutal Wimbledon draw as Brit in desperate fight to be fit for All England Club
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu faces brutal Wimbledon draw as Brit in desperate fight to be fit for All England Club The Sun
Tennis.body:
EMMA RADUCANU could face world No1 Aryna Sabalenka in round three of Wimbledon for the second year running – if she is fit to play. There are major concerns about whether Britain’s tennis superstar will be ready to play next week at the 139th Championships.

For the fourth successive day, she did not train on site at Aorangi Park, having chosen in the morning not to book a practice court for Friday. While she could be practising off-site, the former US Open winner, 23, is managing a niggle in her shin area – there are reports she needs an orthopedic support boot – and faces a race against time to appear at SW19 for the fifth time.

Tennis.title: Home Hope Jack Draper Gets Brutal Opener As Top Guns Learn Path To Wimbledon Title
Tennis.summary: Home Hope Jack Draper Gets Brutal Opener As Top Guns Learn Path To Wimbledon Title ubitennis.net
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper will have to be at the top of his game at Wimbledon after being drawn to play sixth seed Taylor Fritz in the first round. The former world No.4 heads into his home major event having played just one Tour-level event since April due to injury. He is currently playing in Eastbourne, where he is through to the semi-finals. Since playing at Wimbledon last year, Draper has been sidelined by bone bruising in his serving arm before struggling with a knee problem earlier this year.

“I was on a great trajectory last year, being at a career high ranking of four,” Draper said following his quarter-final win at Eastbourne. “My body stopped me in my tracks, so I wasn’t able to play, and the last year has been really difficult.

Tennis.title: How to be a good tennis parent
Tennis.summary: Pushy parents are well documented in tennis - BBC Sport looks at when things get out of hand and what is being done to change this.
Tennis.body:
By the time Ellie-Rose Griffiths was nine, she had left school to train full-time. That was when tennis stopped being just a game and became her life. The former top-ranked junior player would go on to compete alongside some of the top names in British tennis including Katie Boulter, Emma Raducanu and Harriet Dart before stopping playing at 19 because she was burned out and not enjoying it any more.

When the 27-year-old looks back now, it is not just the tennis she remembers. It is the pressure around it, and in particular one group of people she believes could deal with it better.

Tennis.title: Wimbledon draw: Emma Raducanu could face world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka in third round
Tennis.summary: Wimbledon draw: Emma Raducanu could face world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka in third round ESPN
Tennis.body:
WIMBLEDON -- Brit Jack Draper has been drawn to face American sixth seed Taylor Fritz in the first round at Wimbledon. Draper, who is making his way back from a series of injurie in recent months, is in the semifinals of the Eastbourne tournament and faces a tough start to his campaign at the All England Club this year against U.S. star Fritz, who reached the semifinal at Wimbledon last year.

Two-time Wimbledon champion Andy Murray will be alongside Draper next week, having joined the 24-year-old's coaching team. In the women's draw, Emma Raducanu could face world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka in this year's Wimbledon third round, but questions remain over the Brit's fitness heading into the tournament.

Tennis.title: Wimbledon 2026: Jack Draper draws Taylor Fritz, Emma Raducanu on path to meet Aryna Sabalenka & Brits draw more seeds
Tennis.summary: Wimbledon 2026: Jack Draper draws Taylor Fritz, Emma Raducanu on path to meet Aryna Sabalenka & Brits draw more seeds LTA
Tennis.body:
The singles draws have been announced for The Championships, Wimbledon 2026, including a tough first round for Jack Draper, a potential all-British clash followed by a meeting with the world No.1 for Emma Raducanu, and several seeded players for the British wild cards.

Draper has arguably the hardest first round draw of any British player in the Wimbledon draw this year – taking on sixth seed and last year’s semi-finalist Taylor Fritz. The Briton missed out on a seeding having spent a long time out with injuries since Wimbledon last year. After making the quarter-finals at Indian Wells this year, he was hampered by a knee injury before making a return at the Lexus Eastbourne Open this week.

Tennis.title: Williams given favourable Wimbledon start
Tennis.summary: American superstar Serena Williams will make her highly-anticipated singles comeback at Wimbledon against Australia's Maya Joint in the first round.
Tennis.body:
American superstar Serena Williams will make her highly-anticipated singles comeback at Wimbledon against Australian world number 53 Maya Joint in the first round. Williams, who is a seven-time women's champion at the All England Club, has been given a wildcard to restart her singles career at the age of 44, having not played competitively since the US Open in 2022.

Joint, 20, lost in the first round on her Wimbledon main-draw debut last year and has won just one of her past 14 matches. British number one Emma Raducanu, seeded 30th, is set to start against Croatia's Antonia Ruzic, but there are concerns over her fitness after she did not practise on Thursday because of a shin injury.

Tennis.title: Is Serena making her singles comeback too soon?
Tennis.summary: Is Serena making her singles comeback too soon? Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Is Serena Williams brave to make a Wimbledon singles comeback? Can 23-time Grand Slam champion compete at the age of 44? Gamechanger, trailblazer, history-maker: Is Serena brave to make a singles comeback at Wimbledon after only playing two doubles matches?

Ever since it emerged late last year that she had re-entered the anti-doping testing pool - an intrusive and time-consuming commitment that no-one not intending to play professional tennis would put themselves through - the countdown clock has been ticking.

Tennis.title: Chris Evert: Tennis legend undergoes surgery after ovarian cancer returns and will not attend Wimbledon this year
Tennis.summary: Chris Evert: Tennis legend undergoes surgery after ovarian cancer returns and will not attend Wimbledon this year Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Tennis legend Chris Evert has undergone surgery following the return of her ovarian cancer and will not attend Wimbledon this year. The American won 18 Grand Slam singles titles between 1974 and 1986, with only Serena Williams (23) and Steffi Graf (22) claiming more women's singles majors in the Open era.

Player-turned-pundit Evert, 71, was first diagnosed with ovarian cancer in December 2021 and it returned in 2023. Writing on Instagram on Thursday, the three-time Wimbledon champion said: "I have always believed in being open and honest about my health journey.

Tennis.title: Wimbledon: Emma Raducanu emerges as doubt for Grand Slam after missing practice session due to leg injury
Tennis.summary: Wimbledon: Emma Raducanu emerges as doubt for Grand Slam after missing practice session due to leg injury Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu is facing a race to be fit for Wimbledon after missing a practice session on Thursday, Sky Sports News understands. The British No 1 has not played a match since reaching the final at Queen's Club a week-and-a-half ago, choosing not to play in Nottingham or Eastbourne.

She practised on Monday but was spotted in a protective boot on her right foot on Wednesday evening. The 23-year-old was listed on the practice schedule for 12pm on Thursday but Sky Sports News understands she did not show up and is managing a niggle after Queen's.

Tennis.title: Wimbledon build-up: News, updates, with Emma Raducanu, Jack Draper, Jannik Sinner, Aryna Sabalenka, Serena Williams preparing for Grand Slam
Tennis.summary: Wimbledon build-up: News, updates, with Emma Raducanu, Jack Draper, Jannik Sinner, Aryna Sabalenka, Serena Williams preparing for Grand Slam Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Brits Oliver Tarvet, Max Basing and Billy Harris sealed spots in the men’s singles draw at Wimbledon but compatriot Heather Watson was unable to qualify for the women’s event.

Tarvet (pictured below), who reached round two at Wimbledon last year before losing to Carlos Alcaraz on Centre Court, beat Greek 31st seed Stefanos Sakellaridis at Roehampton to make the tournament proper and guarantee himself at least £80,000.

Tennis.summary: Britain's Jack Draper is through to the Eastbourne Open semi-finals after beating Canada's Gabriel Diallo 6-1 6-4. Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Britain's Jack Draper is through to the Eastbourne Open semi-finals after beating Canada's Gabriel Diallo 6-1 6-4. Having endured an injury-ravaged couple of seasons, Draper secured his first victory since March earlier in the tournament, and proved to strong for the six-foot-eight Diallo.

The 24-year-old is working with coach Andy Murray during the grass-court season, with positive preparations for Wimbledon continuing - the Grand Slam beginning on Monday. - Not got Sky? Get Sky Sports or stream with no contract

Tennis.title: Tarvet among three Brits to reach Wimbledon through qualifying
Tennis.summary: A trio of Britons - Ollie Tarvet, Billy Harris and Max Basing - qualify for the Wimbledon main draw.
Tennis.body:
Ollie Tarvet, Billy Harris and Max Basing have reached the Wimbledon main draw, marking the first time since 1999 that three British players have progressed from qualifying. Tarvet, 22, defeated Greece's Stefanos Sakellaridis 6-4 6-2 7-5 for his required third victory of the week in Roehampton and becomes only the third British player to qualify in back-to-back years.

Last year, he came through qualifying to reach the second round at the All England Club, where he was beaten by defending champion Carlos Alcaraz. At the time, he was studying in the United States and American college rules meant he could not claim his full prize money of £99,000.

Tennis.title: Tennis legend Evert says 'relentless' cancer has returned
Tennis.summary: Former Wimbledon champion Chris Evert says her "relentless" cancer has returned for a third time.
Tennis.body:
Former Wimbledon champion Chris Evert says her "relentless" cancer has returned for a third time. The 71-year-old American was first diagnosed with ovarian cancer in December 2021 and says she is hopeful of making a full recovery.

Evert, who won Wimbledon three times and a total of 18 Grand Slams, has been a regular television pundit for the BBC and other broadcasters since retiring in 1989. "I have always believed in being open and honest about my health journey," she wrote on Instagram., external

Tennis.title: Returning Draper reaches Eastbourne semi-finals
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper says he is gaining confidence in his body after he extends his winning return from injury by reaching the Eastbourne Open semi-finals.
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper says he is gaining confidence in his body after extending his winning return from injury by reaching the Eastbourne Open semi-finals with another encouraging straight-set victory.

Contesting his first tournament in more than two months after he sustained a knee injury in April's Barcelona Open, Britain's former world number four Draper beat Canada's Gabriel Diallo for his third straight win on the south coast this week.

Tennis.title: Raducanu misses Wimbledon practice amid injury concern
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu misses training at Wimbledon on Thursday as she manages an injury aggravated by her run to the Queen's final.
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu missed training at Wimbledon on Thursday as she manages an injury aggravated by her run to the Queen's final. The 23-year-old Briton finished as runner-up to Croatia's Donna Vekic after five matches in a week earlier this month, and wore strapping on her left thigh after a slip in the quarter-finals.

But the thigh is not the concern this week, with sources indicating that Raducanu has a shin problem. She was reportedly, external seen leaving the All England Club wearing an orthopaedic boot on her right leg on Wednesday.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu in pre-Wimbledon injury scare after missing training and being spotted in protective boot
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu in pre-Wimbledon injury scare after missing training and being spotted in protective boot lbc.co.uk
Tennis.body:
It has been another stop-start season for the former US Open champion, who missed two and a half months with a post-viral illness after falling unwell in February. Emma Raducanu is facing a race to be fit for Wimbledon after missing training on Thursday.

The British number one has not played a match since reaching the final of the HSBC Championships at Queen’s Club a week and a half ago, choosing not to play in Nottingham or Eastbourne.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu misses training to spark Wimbledon injury fears
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu misses training to spark Wimbledon injury fears The Independent
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu’s participation at Wimbledon has been thrown into doubt after the British No 1 was reportedly spotted at the All England Club wearing an orthopaedic boot. Clay reported that the British No 1 left Aorangi Park, the Wimbledon practice complex, on Wednesday evening wearing a boot on her right leg, while she did not train there when she had been scheduled to at midday on Thursday.

Thomas Houchin, one of her representatives, told Clay: “Emma is absolutely fine. She will play tomorrow”, but her use of the boot just five days before her home grand slam begins has sparked concern.

Tennis.title: Are tennis players right to protest over prize money?
Tennis.summary: Leading tennis players will continue their prize money protest at Wimbledon - what are they asking for and are their demands reasonable?
Tennis.body:
Many of the world's top tennis players will continue their prize money protest by further restricting media opportunities at Wimbledon - but are they right to? After all, it was just a couple of weeks ago that Wimbledon announced a 20% increase in prize money - taking the total sum of the pot to £64.2m.

Leading players, including world number ones Aryna Sabalenka and Jannik Sinner, will not only limit the time they offer at the forthcoming media weekend, but are also planning to restrict post-match appearances to 15 minutes throughout the first week of the Championships, which start on Monday.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu faces race to be fit for Wimbledon after missing practice
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu faces race to be fit for Wimbledon after missing practice The Guardian
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu faces a race to be at full fitness for Wimbledon after her preparations for the tournament were disrupted by injury. On Wednesday evening Raducanu was seen by Clay magazine leaving the All England Club wearing a protective boot on her right foot after not practising that day. She had been scheduled to train at midday on Thursday but did not practise for a second straight day.

Raducanu’s representatives later confirmed that she has been managing a niggle since her run to the final at the Queen’s Club earlier this month. She has not competed in the two tournament weeks since that event.

Tennis.title: Lexus Eastbourne Open 2026: Jack Draper & Toby Samuel reach historic semi-finals
Tennis.summary: Lexus Eastbourne Open 2026: Jack Draper & Toby Samuel reach historic semi-finals LTA
Tennis.body:
Former world No.4 Jack Draper and rising star Toby Samuel are both through to the Lexus Eastbourne Open semi-finals – making it two British players in the final four of the men’s singles.

Draper’s injury comeback took another impressive step forward as he brushed aside Canadian Gabriel Diallo 6-1, 6-4 to make the Eastbourne semi-final for a second time. Samuel, meanwhile, continued his fairytale journey in Eastbourne with a 6-3, 6-4 win over eighth seed Juan Manuel Cerunolo – his first victory over a top-50 opponent.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu: Former coach Mark Petchey believes British No 1 can thrive again under Andrew Richardson
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu: Former coach Mark Petchey believes British No 1 can thrive again under Andrew Richardson Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Former coach Mark Petchey believes Emma Raducanu can win more Grand Slam titles if she chases happiness rather than ranking points and stops worrying about her critics. A year ago, Petchey was in the middle of a successful short-term spell guiding the 23-year-old, but his broadcasting commitments meant it could not become permanent.

Raducanu is now back with Andrew Richardson, the coach who helped her to the US Open title in 2021 that launched her to superstardom but has made the intervening five years a wild and challenging ride.

Tennis.title: Glasgow’s Maia Lumsden is supporting Sir Andy Murray and Jack Draper
Tennis.summary: Glasgow’s Maia Lumsden is supporting Sir Andy Murray and Jack Draper Ealing Times
Tennis.body:
Glasgow’s Maia Lumsden is supporting Sir Andy Murray and Jack Draper in the 2026 Lexus Eastbourne Open, as the Scottish sporting legend backs the former World No.4 on the South Coast.

Lumsden, 28, from Bearsden, progressed into the second round of the WTA 250 tournament with a 3-6 6-4 10-4 victory over Maria Kozyreva and Katarzyna Piter, with her Dutch partner Isabelle Haverlag.

Tennis.title: Eastbourne tennis: Jack Draper issues health update ahead of Wimbledon
Tennis.summary: Eastbourne tennis: Jack Draper issues health update ahead of Wimbledon SussexWorld
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper revealed he battled a stomach bug during his straight-sets victory over close friend Jack Pinnington-Jones at Eastbourne, while insisting there are no fresh concerns over the arm injury that disrupted his season.

The former world number four reached the quarter-finals at Devonshire Park, with a 7-5, 6-4 win over his fellow Surrey player on Wednesday (June 24). Draper was seen wearing a compression sleeve on his left arm throughout the match, prompting questions over whether the issue was related to the injury problems that have hampered him over the past year. However, the 24-year-old quickly dismissed any concerns.

Tennis.title: BBC signs new Wimbledon deal until 2033
Tennis.summary: BBC Sport will continue to broadcast Wimbledon until 2033 after signing a new deal with the All England Club.
Tennis.body:
BBC Sport will continue to broadcast Wimbledon until 2033 after signing a new deal with the All England Club. The agreement means the Grand Slam tournament will remain free to air for audiences in the UK across BBC television, radio and digital platforms.

Alex Kay-Jelski, Director of BBC Sport, said: "Wimbledon holds a truly special place in the hearts of audiences across the UK and this new agreement means we can continue our longstanding and deeply valued partnership with the All England Club well into the next decade.

Tennis.title: Eastbourne Open: Jack Draper defeats fellow Brit Jack Pinnington Jones to advance to quarter-finals
Tennis.summary: Eastbourne Open: Jack Draper defeats fellow Brit Jack Pinnington Jones to advance to quarter-finals Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper overcame a stomach bug and scorching temperatures to defeat close friend Jack Pinnington Jones at the Lexus Eastbourne Open. In baking conditions on the south coast, Draper booked a place in the quarter-finals of the Wimbledon warm-up event with a 7-5 6-4 victory as he continued his comeback from persistent arm and knee injuries.

Victory in an hour and a half maintained his encouraging start under new coach Andy Murray and will see the former world No 4 will face Canada's Gabriel Diallo on Thursday for a spot in the semi-finals.

Tennis.title: Wimbledon 2026: All England Club ‘surprised and disappointed’ as leading players will continue prize money protests
Tennis.summary: Wimbledon 2026: All England Club ‘surprised and disappointed’ as leading players will continue prize money protests Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
The All England Club say are "surprised and disappointed" after the world's leading players announced they will continue to protest the allocation of Grand Slam prize funds at the upcoming Wimbledon Grand Slam.

Earlier this month it was announced that players will benefit from record prize-money increases at this year's event, with the total prize pot rising by 20 per cent (£10.7m) to £64.2m - the largest in the tournament's history.

Tennis.title: An orthopaedic boot raises doubts over Emma Raducanu at Wimbledon
Tennis.summary: An orthopaedic boot raises doubts over Emma Raducanu at Wimbledon claytenis.com
Tennis.body:
LONDON — Emma Raducanu has cast doubt over her participation at Wimbledon after she was spotted at the All England Club wearing an orthopaedic boot. On Wednesday at around six in the evening, the British star left Aorangi Park — Wimbledon’s official practice and warm-up area — with a grey immobilisation boot on her right leg.

According to information provided to CLAY at the tournament, Raducanu did not practise today. The 2021 US Open champion is, however, scheduled to train this Thursday at midday on Court 10 in the northern section of the complex.

According to information provided to CLAY at the tournament, Raducanu did not practise today. The 2021 US Open champion is, however, scheduled to train this Thursday at midday on Court 10 in the northern section of the complex.

Tennis.title: Draper into last eight to continue injury comeback
Tennis.summary: Britain's Jack Draper says his recent injury problems left his confidence levels "below the floor" before he reached the quarter-finals in Eastbourne.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Jack Draper says his recent injury problems left his confidence levels "below the floor". But the 24-year-old continued his comeback with an assured win over compatriot Jack Pinnington Jones to reach the Eastbourne quarter-finals.

Draper played his first competitive match in more than two months on Monday after a knee injury sidelined him in April's Barcelona Open. That was the latest in a long list of injury issues which meant he has played just 13 matches in the past year.

Tennis.title: 'I love Eastbourne, thank you guys' - Jack Draper continues comeback with big win amid vocal support from Andy Murray
Tennis.summary: 'I love Eastbourne, thank you guys' - Jack Draper continues comeback with big win amid vocal support from Andy Murray SussexWorld
Tennis.body:
Home favourite Jack Draper booked his place in the Eastbourne quarter-finals with a straight-sets victory over fellow Brit Jack Pinnington Jones, winning 7-5, 6-4 in an entertaining battle.

Draper, 24, was forced to work hard for the win and was broken while serving for the opening set at 5-4, having also failed to close out his previous-round match against Marcos Giron at the first attempt.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper Reaches Last Eight Of Comeback Event In Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper Reaches Last Eight Of Comeback Event In Eastbourne ubitennis.net
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper has recorded back-to-back wins on the Tour for the first time since March after beating compatriot Jack Pinnington Jones in the second round of the Eastbourne Open.

Draper, who until this week had been sidelined from action for two months due to a right knee tendon injury, edged out Pinnington Jones 7-5, 6-4. The Brit was given a stern test during the match, especially towards the end of the first set, when he was broken while serving at 5-4. Despite the blip, Draper regained control of the match by winning four consecutive games and didn’t face a break point throughout the second set.

Tennis.body:
Former world No.4 Jack Draper’s impressive comeback continues after he overcame fellow British star Jack Pinnington Jones to reach the quarter-finals at the Lexus Eastbourne Open.

Draper continued his winning return to action by clinching another straight sets victory over compatriot and good friend Pinnington Jones - winning 7-5, 6-4 to reach the final eight in Eastbourne for the second time in as many appearances.

Tennis.body:
Leading tennis players will expand their prize money protest at Wimbledon - despite a 20% increase in this year's pot. Some players limited pre-tournament media to 15 minutes at the recent French Open, but will go a step further at Wimbledon.

They will not only limit the time they offer at the forthcoming media weekend, but are also planning to restrict post-match appearances to 15 minutes throughout the first week of the Championships, which start on Monday.

Tennis.title: GB's Evans out of Wimbledon qualifying as singles career ends
Tennis.summary: Dan Evans' singles career is over after losing to Tristan Schoolkate in the second round of Wimbledon qualifying.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Dan Evans has played the last professional singles match of his career after losing to Tristan Schoolkate in the second round of Wimbledon qualifying. The 36-year-old announced earlier this month that he would retire after the Championships, which starts on Monday.

Evans missed out on one of the eight singles wildcards, meaning he needed to win three matches in Roehampton this week to play one last singles tournament at SW19. But his singles career came to a disappointing end as he suffered a 7-5 6-0 defeat in a match that was disrupted by an issue with the electronic line calling system.

Tennis.summary: Eastbourne Tennis: 29 brilliant crowd and player pics - Jack Draper and Andy Murray SussexWorld
Tennis.body:
Eastbourne Tennis week continues to draw the crowds at Devonshire Park. Star players such Jack Draper and Madison Keys provided the entertainment while the baking hot weather had the crowd seeking shady spots and reaching for their fans. Sir Andy Murray was also on site as he continues to coach Draper for the grass court swing.

There was also plenty of British interest as Draper, Jacob Fearnley, Arthur Fery, Jack Pinnington Jones contested the men’s singles and Francesca Jones, Harriet Dart, Alicia Dudeney and Hannah Klugman were in the women’s.

Tennis.title: Eastbourne Tennis 2026: LTA provides weather warning ahead of Jack Draper clash
Tennis.summary: Eastbourne Tennis 2026: LTA provides weather warning ahead of Jack Draper clash SussexWorld
Tennis.body:
The LTA said there are currently no plans in place to adjust today’s schedule of play at a baking hot Devonshire Park. Severe weather warnings are in place in Eastbourne, the south coast and across large parts of the UK.

Tennis fans getting to Eastbourne could also face disruption and Southern Rail advised to “only travel if absolutely necessary on Wednesday and Thursday.” They also added trains will be busier and journeys will take longer and advised customers to visit the Southern website for latest travel advice and ticket information before setting off. Friday’s travel remains under review.
Tennis.copy:
The LTA said there are currently no plans in place to adjust today’s schedule of play at a baking hot Devonshire Park. Severe weather warnings are in place in Eastbourne, the south coast and across large parts of the UK.

Tennis fans getting to Eastbourne could also face disruption and Southern Rail advised to “only travel if absolutely necessary on Wednesday and Thursday.” They also added trains will be busier and journeys will take longer and advised customers to visit the Southern website for latest travel advice and ticket information before setting off. Friday’s travel remains under review.

Tennis.title: Tim Henman Backs Emma Raducanu to Win Another Grand Slam: 'She Can Do It Again' - Tennis Temple
Tennis.summary: Tim Henman Backs Emma Raducanu to Win Another Grand Slam: 'She Can Do It Again' Tennis Temple - Live tennis
Tennis.body:
With Wimbledon approaching (June 29 to July 12), excitement is once again building around Emma Raducanu in the UK. Since her unexpected breakthrough in the summer of 2021, capped by her US Open triumph, the Briton has carried the hopes of a nation yearning for a local champion to win Wimbledon—a feat not achieved by a British woman since Virginia Wade in 1977.

Indeed, expectations are high again after her impressive run at the Queen's tournament. Returning to competition after several weeks off due to a virus, the 23-year-old showed encouraging form by reaching the final.

Tennis.title: Anisimova plots Wimbledon sequel with happier ending
Tennis.summary: Many wondered how long it would take Amanda Anisimova to recover from last year's Wimbledon final. About six weeks was the answer.
Tennis.body:
Amanda Anisimova will arrive at Wimbledon this year with plenty of good memories - but also in the hope she can "rewrite the story" with a much happier ending. Twelve months ago, Anisimova won a compelling two-and-a-half hour tussle in the semi-finals with world number one Aryna Sabalenka to reach a first Grand Slam final.

But just two days later she failed to win a single game in the showpiece against Iga Swiatek, losing 6-0 6-0 in only 57 minutes - a scoreline that had not been seen in a Wimbledon ladies' singles final since 1911.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu launches eco-friendly vegan deodorant ‘inspired by Kent upbringing’
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu launches eco-friendly vegan deodorant ‘inspired by Kent upbringing’ The Telegraph
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu has launched an eco-friendly vegan deodorant which she says is “inspired” by her upbringing in Kent. Raducanu, who sparked talk of a pre-Wimbledon relationship last week after being spotted in a clinch with a PR expert, unveiled a range of personal hygiene products on Tuesday known as “The Garden of England” collection.

It has been created in conjunction with Wild, a cosmetics company that describes itself as “the UK’s No 1 refillable bathroom brand”. The company aims to reduce plastic waste and also boasts of being vegan.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu would lose Wimbledon seeding amid controversial rule change proposal
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu would lose Wimbledon seeding amid controversial rule change proposal talkSPORT
Tennis.body:
A shake-up to the seedings at Wimbledon has been proposed that would see the number dramatically slashed in half. The idea, proposed by British tennis stalwarts John Lloyd and Andrew Castle, would see the number of seeds at Wimbledon go from 32 to just 16.

Had that been in place in 2026 then British number one Emma Raducanu would have felt the effects. The 23-year-old is set to be seeded at No32 at this year's tournament, which begins on June 29.

Tennis.title: GB's Adeshina two wins away from Wimbledon dream
Tennis.summary: Britain's Esther Adeshina beats Japan's Moyuka Uchijima for the biggest win of her career to move within two victories of a spot in the main draw at Wimbledon.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Esther Adeshina said it was a "dream" to be playing in Wimbledon qualifying after achieving the biggest win of her career to move within two victories of a place in the main draw.

The 24-year-old beat Japan's former top-50 player Moyuka Uchijima 6-1 3-6 7-5 in after almost two hours in stifling heat in Roehampton. World number 514 Adeshina, will next face France's Harmony Tan, who famously beat Serena Williams on her way to the Wimbledon fourth round in 2022.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper and Axel Arigato’s partnership makes tennis more stylish than ever
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper and Axel Arigato’s partnership makes tennis more stylish than ever Culted
Tennis.body:
For years, Jack Draper was known primarily as British tennis’ next great hope. As one of the brightest prospects to emerge from British tennis in the post-Andy Murray era, Draper’s journey has often been measured through rankings, tournament runs and the constant pressure that comes with carrying a nation’s hopes. Yet, as his game has matured, so too has the person behind it.

Today, Draper occupies a rare space in modern sport. He’s one of the most exciting players on the ATP Tour, while he’s also part of a generation stepping into different mediums outside of their sport. Of course, performance is always the priority (especially with Wimbledon around the corner) but personality, culture and self-expression have become increasingly important parts of the equation.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper Reflect On 'Inspirations' From Andy Murray After Successful ATP Return In Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper Reflect On 'Inspirations' From Andy Murray After Successful ATP Return In Eastbourne ubitennis.net
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper spoke about how inspirational Andy Murray has been as he made a successful start to their partnership. The three-time Grand Slam champion is now aiding Draper’s comeback as the Brit played his first match in two months on Monday.

It was a winning return for Draper who overcame Marcos Giron 6-4 7-6 and is into the second round in Eastbourne. The Brit will now face compatriot and good friend Jack Pinnington Jones after Pinnington Jones saved match point to defeat Marco Trungelliti.

Tennis.title: How is UK heatwave impacting sport for players and fans?
Tennis.summary: BBC Sport explores the impact of the UK heatwave on sports events across the country
Tennis.body:
An unprecedented heatwave is beginning in the UK, with the June temperature record almost certainly set to be broken over the next few days. The current record high of 35.6C, set in 1976, is set to be surpassed as meteorologists forecast temperatures as high as 38C and 39C in some parts of the country.

The Met Office has issued a rare red warning for extreme heat for sections of England and Wales from 09:00 BST on Wednesday until 21:00 on Thursday. The red warning area includes London, Cardiff, the south west and south east, the Midlands, and the east of England.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu, global ambassador for Wild, launches her own scent with the brand
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu, global ambassador for Wild, launches her own scent with the brand Retail Times
Tennis.body:
Refillable personal care brand Wild and its Global Brand Ambassador, Emma Raducanu, have teamed up to create her very own scent, Garden of England. Inspired by her upbringing in Kent, Garden of England blends crisp rhubarb, fresh green leaves and rose petals, capturing the feeling of a British summer in the countryside.

The launch marks the latest milestone in Emma’s partnership with Wild, which began in 2025. As an athlete, Emma puts Wild through its paces every day, demonstrating that high-performance protection and natural formulas can go hand in hand.

Tennis.title: Everything to know about Wimbledon's main warm-up event and if it's worth going
Tennis.summary: Everything to know about Wimbledon's main warm-up event and if it's worth going North Wales Chronicle
Tennis.body:
I got the chance to spend a day at the main warm-up tournament to Wimbledon, but is it worth going? Wimbledon is a huge event in the tennis calendar, with it being one of the four main Grand Slam events along with the Australian Open, French Open and US Open.

As part of the build-up to the tournament, players will take part in a few smaller competitions. One of these is The Queen's Club Championships, which is held at the Queen's Club in West Kensington, London.

Tennis.title: Battling egos and stereotypes
Tennis.summary: In a sport like tennis, which prides itself on gender equality, why are there still so few female coaches and what is being done to address the issue?
Tennis.body:
The coaching box is one of the most visible places in tennis. Players often glance towards it after every point. Television cameras pan to it dozens of times during a match. Commentators spend time trying to dissect the reactions of those within it.

But if you look closely, whether at regular tour events or at Wimbledon in the coming fortnight, you will usually see most of the coaching staff inside it are men. Female coaches, even at the top of the women's game, are a rarity. Only four players inside the top 50 in singles have a woman as their primary coach.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper is turning Wimbledon's “energy” into a sneaker
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper is turning Wimbledon's “energy” into a sneaker British GQ
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper's ascent is difficult to ignore. At 24, the British tennis star has established himself as one of the biggest names on the ATP tour. And somewhere between all the training blocks and Grand Slam campaigns, he's become a bit of a sneaker designer as well.

The latest chapter of his ongoing collab with Axel Arigato is Rise, the second release in a three-part trilogy developed alongside the Swedish label. Following Dusk (which drew inspo from the clay courts of Roland-Garros), Rise turns its attention a lot closer to home.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu: ‘Learning On The Public Stage Has Been A Huge Challenge’
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu: ‘Learning On The Public Stage Has Been A Huge Challenge’ Grazia Daily UK
Tennis.body:
The first time I interviewed Emma Raducanu she was just 19. It was barely a year after she had blasted on to the world stage with her shock US Open win – the first British woman to win a major singles championship since 1977 – and her reservation at answering my questions was palpable. But today, sitting across from the now 23-year-old tennis star, she has a new energy. Relaxed and self-assured, she's come a long way from the teenager who appeared overwhelmed by the weight of everyone else's expectations.

'I didn't have the backbone [back then],' she says. 'I would try and justify a certain answer in my head because I thought that was the "right" thing to do or say or the "right" person to work with, even if it didn't feel right. People were telling me it was a good idea and I found it hard to remove myself from situations even if I knew it was wrong. Every mistake or decision was up for scrutiny, so you're learning on the go, but on a public stage. I wish I had trusted my instincts more. In the last few years, I've been more comfortable being open and honest.'

Tennis.title: The Andy Murray factor that helped injury-plagued Jack Draper get back to ‘full power’ at Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: The Andy Murray factor that helped injury-plagued Jack Draper get back to ‘full power’ at Eastbourne The Independent
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper says Andy Murray’s impact has fired him back to his “powerful” best after a triumphant return to competitive tennis at Eastbourne. The former World No 4, who has plummeted down the rankings after an injury-ridden few months, clinched a first-round victory over American Marcos Giron 6-4 7-6 (7-5) in front of new coach Murray on the south coast.

Draper, a former US Open semi-finalist, cut a restless figure in Devonshire Park after a series of chronic arm and knee injuries had sidelined him since April. But the 24-year-old year dusted off the cobwebs in style to warm up for a Wimbledon return in perfect style and set up a last 16 date with fellow Brit Jack Pinnington Jones.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper hails new coach Andy Murray after winning return at Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper hails new coach Andy Murray after winning return at Eastbourne The Guardian
Tennis.body:
An amusing question at the beginning of this new, hopeful chapter in Jack Draper’s career was tendered to the 24-year-old without hesitation before his return to competition in Eastbourne this week. In the heat of a dispiriting, stressful battle on court, when things are going badly and he might need to discharge some of his tension, would he ever allow himself to scream at Andy Murray?

The prospect of hollering expletives at his idol, friend and new coach drew laughter from Draper. Then he shook his head: “I don’t think so,” Draper said. “Maybe a few times I’ve said something to my team but it’s mainly anger at myself. I’ve never been someone who goes off at my time, because I have a great relationship with all of them. I have too much respect for Andy and Trots [James Trotman, his other coach] to be doing that.”

Tennis.title: Eastbourne Open: Britain's Jack Draper defeats Marcos Giron in first match under new coach Andy Murray
Tennis.summary: Eastbourne Open: Britain's Jack Draper defeats Marcos Giron in first match under new coach Andy Murray Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper beat lucky loser Marcos Giron 6-4 7-6 (7-5) in an impressive straight sets first-round victory at the Eastbourne Open in his first game under new coach Andy Murray.

Draper has been hampered by injuries since the US Open last August, with a knee problem causing his latest stint on the sidelines since April. The former World No 4 returned on the grass of Eastbourne after only playing nine matches throughout 2026 one week before Wimbledon starts with British tennis great Murray as part of his coaching set-up for the first time in competition.

Tennis.title: Draper 'wins ugly like Murray' on injury return
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper says he drew inspiration from new coach Sir Andy Murray to claim an "ugly" win in his first competitive match in over two months.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Jack Draper says he drew inspiration from new coach Sir Andy Murray to claim an "ugly" win in his first competitive match in over two months. A knee injury has kept the 24-year-old on the sidelines since April, but he has been working with Murray in recent weeks after joining up with the three-time Grand Slam champion for the grass-court season.

The pair's partnership got off to a winning start at Eastbourne as Draper held off a late charge by American Marcos Giron to secure a 6-4 7-6 (7-5) victory. Asked what it was like having a knight in his coaching box, Draper joked: "I haven't called him 'Sir' yet - that's not going to happen.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper hails Sir Andy Murray for guiding him to ‘ugly win’ at Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper hails Sir Andy Murray for guiding him to ‘ugly win’ at Eastbourne Somerset County Gazette
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper credited new coach Sir Andy Murray with inspiring him to an “ugly” win on his latest injury comeback. Three-time grand-slam winner Murray watched from a court-side box on Monday as Draper beat American lucky loser Marcos Giron 6-4 7-6 (5) in the first round of the Lexus Eastbourne Open.

The former world number four has been plagued by chronic arm and knee issues during the past year but felt no ill effects during a solid display on a sweltering afternoon at Devonshire Park.

Tennis.title: Ex-Wimbledon champion Vondrousova given four-year ban
Tennis.summary: Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova is banned for four years for refusing an anti-doping test in December 2025.
Tennis.body:
Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been banned for four years for refusing an anti-doping test. Vondrousova, 26, was charged by the International Tennis Integrity Agency (ITIA) after denying a doping control officer entry to her home to conduct the test in December 2025.

The Czech player said in April she feared for her safety when the officer called and said they failed to follow "protocol". But a tribunal determined Vondrousova, who became the first unseeded player to win the Wimbledon women's singles title in 2023, provided "no compelling justification" for refusing a test.

Tennis.title: Draper returns at Eastbourne & Wimbledon qualifying begins
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper makes his return at Eastbourne and Wimbledon qualifying gets under way - follow it across the BBC.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Jack Draper makes his long-awaited return from injury at Eastbourne - and you can watch it live on the BBC. Former world number four Draper beat lucky loser Marcus Giron in his first match for two months, and will face fellow Briton Jack Pinnington Jones in the second round.

Draper is accompanied in Eastbourne by new coach Sir Andy Murray, who has joined the Briton's team for the grass court season. World number nine and four-time winner Taylor Fritz is the highest-ranked player in the draw, which also includes recent Queen's quarter-finalist Arthur Fery and highly-rated Brazilian teenager Joao Fonseca.

Tennis.title: Lexus Eastbourne Open 2026: Jack Draper beats Marcos Giron on injury return in Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Lexus Eastbourne Open 2026: Jack Draper beats Marcos Giron on injury return in Eastbourne LTA
Tennis.body:
Former world No.4 Jack Draper enjoyed a winning return to action on the ATP Tour after securing a straight sets victory over lucky loser Marcos Giron at the Lexus Eastbourne Open.

Draper showed little signs of rustiness in his first round clash against the lucky loser, wrapping up the opening set in just 36 minutes before sealing an impressive 6-4, 7-6(5) victory.
